title: Stelis angelica
intro: Stelis angelica, also known as Angel's Stelis, is a genus of flowering plants in the orchid family, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Stelis angelica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis angelica is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Stelis angelica is a species of orchid in the subtribe Stanhopeinae. It belongs to the genus Stelis and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Stelis is a part of the tribe Epidendreae. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. Stelis angelica is an epiphytic orchid native to tropical regions.
uses: Stelis angelica is known for its potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional medicine, Stelis angelica has been used to treat various conditions, including fever, infections, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. Further research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal potential of Stelis angelica.
